title: Bradford Protein Assay — Standard Curve
author: Bio-Rad Laboratories
citation: Bradford, M.M. (1976). A rapid and sensitive method for the quantitation of microgram quantities of protein using the principle of protein-dye binding. Analytical Biochemistry, 72, 248–254.

steps.title: Prepare BSA standards
steps.instruction:
Prepare a serial dilution of BSA in distilled water to generate concentrations of **0, 125, 250, 500, 750, 1000, 1500, and 2000 µg/mL** from the 2 mg/mL stock.

Use 10 µL of each standard per well.

steps.title: Add Bradford Reagent
steps.instruction:
Add **200 µL of Bradford Coomassie Reagent** to each well containing standard or unknown sample.

> **Critical:** Avoid bubbles — they interfere with absorbance readings.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate at room temperature for **5 minutes**. The reagent will turn blue in the presence of protein.

steps.title: Read absorbance at 595 nm
steps.instruction: Measure absorbance at **595 nm** on a plate reader within **60 minutes** of adding the reagent (colour is stable for 1 hour).

steps.title: Generate standard curve
steps.instruction: Plot absorbance (y-axis) vs. BSA concentration (x-axis). Fit a linear regression. Use the equation to interpolate protein concentrations of your unknowns.
